rulings.Case Title: RICHARD SHAW VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, April 18, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Performance Industries Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is off calendar at the moving party¿s request. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN L. WILLIAMS VS. GEORGIA-PACIFIC CORPORATION
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, April 18, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.¿s motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff¿s claim for punitive damages is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant punitive damages. Plaintiff¿s responses to defendant¿s special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN L. WILLIAMS VS. GEORGIA-PACIFIC CORPORATION
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, April 18, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Hanson Permanente Cement, Inc.¿s motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff¿s claim for punitive damages is denied. Defendant sustained its initial burden of production. Plaintiff submitted sufficient evidence to create a triable issue whether defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Patricia Rodriguez, CSR #12436 = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: FREDERICK RUDY VS. ALTA BUILDING MATERIAL CO.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, April 18, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Hanson Permanente Cement, Inc.¿s motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff¿s claim for punitive damages is denied. Defendant sustained its initial burden of production. Plaintiff submitted sufficient evidence to create a triable issue whether defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Defendant¿s objections to the deposition testimony of Mr. Crosby and Mr. Crum are overruled. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
